rulings.Case Title: DAVID WOLGAMOTT VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 10. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication is now, per the request of the moving party, solely a motion for summary adjudication on plaintiff¿s request for punitive damages. The motion for summary adjudication on punitive damages is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. Defendant¿s evidentiary objections 1-5 are sustained. Assuming without deciding that the excerpts from the workers¿ compensation claims are admissible against authentication objections, those excerpts are not relevant absent information about the facts and circumstances of the workers involved including but not limited to the frequency of their exposure to asbestos-containing materials. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: VINCENT MONTESANO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment and its alternate motion for summary adjudication (exposure to asbestos) are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing packing from Crane Co. valves. Mr. Montesano¿s deposition and written discovery responses and the deposition testimony of Mr. McLean and Mr. Pantaleoni, which were identified and discussed in plaintiff¿s discovery responses, preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Dr. Bruch¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether such exposure was a substantial factor contributing to plaintiff¿s risk of developing an asbestos-related disease. Defendant¿s objections to Dr. Bruch¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: VINCENT MONTESANO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Taylor Plumbing Supply Company¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar per moving party's request. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: VINCENT MONTESANO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant D.W. Nicholson Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Due to defendant¿s failure to submit the special interrogatories it propounded on plaintiff and plaintiff¿s disputing of facts 11, 14 and 16 in defendant¿s separate statement, defendant did not sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant and/or that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD SHARP VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant J-M Manufacturing Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Mr. Sharp¿s deposition testimony and declaration and defendant¿s General Order No. 129 interrogatory responses create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos cement pipe attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Sharp¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD SHARP VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Power Boiler Sales, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. By failing to question plaintiff during his deposition about Peerless boilers, which plaintiff identified in response to defendant¿s special interrogatories, defendant did not sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, plaintiff¿s declaration, defendant¿s responses to General Order No. 129 interrogatories and Mr. Burkhardt¿s deposition testimony create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GARY HALL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Albay Construction Company¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether the insulation, gaskets and/or packing disturbed by defendant¿s employees in plaintiff¿s presence contained asbestos. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM MECHLING VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Crosby Valve, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether any of the original gaskets and/or packing plaintiff removed and/or disturbed from Crosby valves contained asbestos. Defendant¿s objections to the declaration of Mr. Ay are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES MIHALKO et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant SBC Holdings, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 2 (premises liability and civil battery) and taken off calendar as to issue 3 (punitive damages). The declaration of Mr. Mihalko and exhibits A through E to Mr. Noah¿s declaration create a triable issue whether Washington¿s statute of limitations bars plaintiffs¿ claims. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Mihalko was exposed to asbestos-containing maeterials disturbed by defendant. The deposition of Mr. Mihalko precludes the successful invocation of the factually devoid prong of Aguilar on this issue. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES MIHALKO et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production that Mr. Mihalko was diagnosed with an asbestos-related disease in 1997 while he was living in Washington and plaintiffs did not present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue. Defendant¿s evidentiary objections 1-4 are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES MIHALKO et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporations Motion For Summary Judgment Upon The Application Of The Washington Statute Of Limitations
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Amcord, Inc.¿s joinder in Metalclad Insulation Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. One party may not join another party¿s summary judgment motion unless the joining party itself complies with all the procedural requirements for making a summary judgment motion. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES MIHALKO et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Metalclad Insulation Corporations Motion For Summary Judgment Upon The Application Of The Washington Statute Of Limitations
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Syd Carpenter Marine Contractor, Inc.¿s joinder in Metalclad Insulation Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. One party may not join another party¿s summary judgment motion unless the joining party itself complies with all the procedural requirements for making a summary judgment motion. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES MIHALKO et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied as untimely. The prior order allowed summary judgment motions to be heard on 30 days notice. As the trial date is March 14, the last day to hear summary judgment motions is February 10 since February 11 is a court holiday and Department 220 does not hear motions on Fridays. 30 days notice from February 10 required defendant to file and serve its motion on or before January 11. Defendant filed and served its motion on January 12. Defendant¿s Notice of Errata cannot cure this untimeliness. The court lacks the authority to grant defendant¿s request to excuse its untimeliness. = (220/HEK)
